debris_field.name: The Debris Field
debris_field.description: A slowly tumbling mass of grey hull plating, olive-drab cargo containers, and twisted structural steel, spread across a pocket of void. The pieces rotate in silence, catching dull metallic light. The wreckage of a great vessel — shattered, scattered, and cold.
debris_field.aspect: A Dead Ship Still Full of Secrets

rooms.name: Edge of the Debris Field
rooms.description: Jagged shards of grey hull plating drift lazily against the black void, spinning slow enough to catch dull reflections of distant light. The largest pieces — bent girders, crumpled bulkheads — hum faintly, as if the wreckage still remembers being whole. A clear path back to the skerry lies to the south.
aspects: Everything Drifts If You Let Go

rooms.name: Shattered Cargo Bay
rooms.description: The remnants of a cargo hold, its outer walls peeled back like a tin can to expose rust-brown internal plating. Crates of dull green and grey bob gently in the void, some still sealed with corroded latches. Emergency strip-lighting along the ceiling flickers orange in places, casting long shadows between the floating containers.

rooms.name: Gaping Hull Breach
rooms.description: A massive hole torn through the outer hull, its ragged edges dark with corrosion. Layers of internal structure are exposed — insulation foam, conduit bundles, structural ribs — like looking into the cross-section of a body. Sparks flash intermittently from deeper in the wreckage, throwing harsh white light across the twisted metal.

rooms.name: Ruined Control Room
rooms.description: A cramped room ringed with dead consoles, their dark screens cracked and frosted with corrosion. A single emergency light overhead bathes everything in dull amber. Someone has cleared a workspace near the listing captain's chair — tools laid out in precise rows on a sheet of fabric, oil rags folded neatly beside them.
aspects: Just Enough Power to Be Dangerous

rooms.name: Engine Room Wreckage
rooms.description: A cavernous space dominated by massive turbines split open like cracked eggs, their dark cores exposed. Oil has pooled and frozen in black sheets across the deck. Among the wreckage, a cylindrical device the size of a person pulses with faint blue-white light — the Stabilization Engine, still drawing power from something.
aspects: The Heart Stopped But It's Still Warm

enemies_data.name: Rat Swarm
enemies_data.description: A chittering mass of pale, eyeless rats with translucent skin stretched tight over visible bones. They pour from cracks in the bulkhead like liquid, each one small but the whole swarm a gnawing tide.

enemies_data.name: Hound of Annwn
enemies_data.description: A spectral hound with white fur and red-tipped ears, its form flickering between solid and translucent. It watches you with ancient, knowing eyes, and its growl resonates in your chest.
aspects: Between This World and the Next

enemies_data.name: Corroded Automaton
enemies_data.description: A maintenance robot gone wrong. Its corroded frame jerks with erratic, dangerous movements. Sparks fly from exposed wiring.
aspects: Doesn't Know What It's Doing Anymore
One Spark From a Short Circuit

coral_thicket.name: The Coral Thicket
coral_thicket.description: A dense mass of crystallized organic matter in shades of blue, green, pink, and violet, glowing with soft bioluminescence against the void-dark. The coral structures twist and branch in impossible spiraling patterns, creating a labyrinth of sharp beauty. The air around it shimmers with warmth and drifting spores.
coral_thicket.aspect: Everything Here Grows Toward Something Hungry

rooms.name: Coral Threshold
rooms.description: The thicket begins as a wall of pale blue-green crystalline growths, branching outward like fingers against the void-dark. The formations are translucent, lit from within by slow bioluminescent pulses. The air here is warm and faintly sweet, with a mineral edge. Underfoot, the ground transitions from bare rock to a crust of tiny coral nubs.
aspects: Beautiful Enough to Drop Your Guard
The Sweetness Wants Something From You

rooms.name: Luminous Grove
rooms.description: A clearing among the coral where enormous fan-shaped growths arch overhead, filtering light into shifting blue and green dapples across the ground. The space is roughly circular, ten paces wide. A crude lean-to of stacked coral slabs and fabric scraps leans against the largest formation, its entrance hung with a torn curtain.
aspects: Someone Lived Here For a Reason

rooms.name: Narrow Coral Tunnel
rooms.description: A tight passage between walls of dense coral, barely shoulder-width. The formations press in from both sides, their surfaces studded with thorn-like protrusions that scrape fabric and skin. The coral pulses with slow internal light — pink and violet close up — and the air is close and humid.
aspects: No Room to Swing a Sword

rooms.name: Spore Chamber
rooms.description: A vast hollow chamber, high-ceilinged and dark at its edges. Clouds of luminous spores drift through the space like slow-motion snow, each mote glowing soft yellow-green. They pulse in unison — bright, dim, bright — and the air is thick, warm, and sweet enough to taste. The ground is spongy with accumulated organic matter.
aspects: Breathe Wrong and You'll See Things
The Warmth Makes You Want to Stay

rooms.name: Heart of the Thicket
rooms.description: The deepest chamber of the thicket, where the coral formations are ancient and intricately branched — some as tall as trees, their surfaces encrusted with smaller growths in white, pink, and deep violet. A small garden of cultivated coral occupies the center, tended beds marked with careful borders. Among the oldest roots, a lattice of living crystal pulses with steady green light.

enemies_data.name: Thorn Crawler
enemies_data.description: A coral-armored predator that blends with the thicket walls. It strikes from ambush, its barbed limbs lashing out with surprising speed.
aspects: Strikes Before You Know It's There
Part of the Wall Until It Isn't

enemies_data.name: Thorn Crawler Alpha
enemies_data.description: The largest of its kind, this crawler has grown thick coral plating and developed a cunning patience. It waits until prey is committed before striking.
aspects: It Can Wait Longer Than You Can

enemies_data.name: Spore Cloud
enemies_data.description: Not a creature exactly — a living cloud of luminous spores that drifts with purpose. Inhaling them causes disorientation and hallucinations.
aspects: One Breath and the World Goes Sideways
It Goes Where the Air Goes

frozen_wreck.name: The Frozen Wreck
frozen_wreck.description: An ancient ship of dark, seamless metal, encrusted in blue-white void-ice and glittering with frost. It drifts in eerie silence, its angular hull reflecting cold light. The ice is centuries thick in places, translucent enough to hint at shapes preserved within.
frozen_wreck.aspect: The Cold Preserves What Should Have Been Forgotten

rooms.name: Ice-Crusted Bow
rooms.description: The prow of the ancient vessel juts from a mass of blue-white void-ice, its dark metal hull dense and seamless. Frost coats every surface in fine white crystals. The hull is covered in engraved script that catches the light differently depending on the angle — silver, then gold, then gone. The ice creaks and ticks in the silence.
aspects: The Writing Changes When You Look Away
Built By Hands That Knew More Than Ours

rooms.name: Frost-Locked Corridor
rooms.description: A long corridor sheathed in ice so thick the dark metal walls are barely visible beneath. Your breath crystallizes immediately, hanging in white clouds. The ice is milky blue in places, clear as glass in others — and in the clear patches, dark shapes are suspended mid-motion, their outlines too blurred to identify.
aspects: Cold Enough to Steal Your Thoughts
Something Watches From Beneath the Ice

rooms.name: Observation Deck
rooms.description: A domed room walled with panels of translucent crystal, now frosted into translucency. Pale void-light filters through, casting the room in cold blue-grey. The curved ceiling amplifies every sound. A desk near the center is buried under layers of notes, charcoal rubbings, and scraps of metal etched with copied inscriptions.
aspects: The Void Stares Back Through Frosted Glass
Every Surface Covered in Someone's Obsession

rooms.name: Crew Quarters
rooms.description: Rows of alcoves line both walls, receding into dim blue shadow. Each alcove holds a frozen shape — some curled, some rigid, draped in ice that is thinnest here. In places, the outlines of faces and hands press against the translucent surface. The floor is slick and the silence is total.
aspects: One Crack and Everything Comes Through

rooms.name: Ancient Armory
rooms.description: Weapon racks line the walls in orderly rows, but their contents have fused with centuries of ice — blades and hafts locked into crystalline sheaths. The room is long and low-ceilinged, lit by the ambient glow of the ice itself. One corner has been cleared of frost, a bedroll and small fire-pit set up among the frozen arsenal.
aspects: Someone Here Is Ready for a Fight

rooms.name: The Vault
rooms.description: The deepest chamber, perfectly circular and high-vaulted. The ice here is clear as glass, and through it the walls reveal intricate murals in faded gold and green — a civilization of tree-like beings, their branching limbs raised in gestures of ceremony or worship. At the center, encased in a pillar of flawless crystal, rests a small structure no larger than two cupped hands — perfect, ornate, unmistakably a house.
aspects: The Walls Tell a Dead People's Story

enemies_data.name: Frost Shade
enemies_data.description: A shadow that moves beneath the ice, reaching through with freezing tendrils. It was something once — crew, passenger, prisoner. Now it's just cold and hunger.
aspects: Reaches Through What Should Be Solid

enemies_data.name: Swarm Mites
enemies_data.description: Tiny crystalline insects that swarm in vast numbers. Each one is fragile, but together they can strip metal to the bone.
aspects: Tiny Jaws That Strip Metal Bare

enemies_data.name: Frost Shade Elder
enemies_data.description: The captain of the frozen wreck, preserved in void-ice for centuries. Its form is massive, translucent, and filled with a terrible cold intelligence. It guards the vault and the Eliok House within.
aspects: Still Thinks This Ship Is Its Command
Centuries of Cold Made It Patient
Nothing Leaves the Vault Without Permission

verdant_wreck.name: The Verdant Wreck
verdant_wreck.description: A massive biodome fragment — the growing section of a colony ship, its transparent dome panels fogged with condensation. When the ship broke apart, the growth systems kept running. Now it drifts in the void as a small jungle of dense green canopy and humid air, warm light leaking from every seam and crack in the hull.

rooms.name: Overgrown Airlock
rooms.description: Thick brown roots crack through grey hull plates, splitting seams that were never meant to open. Warm, humid air rushes outward carrying the smell of wet soil and chlorophyll. The airlock frame is half-buried in bright green moss, and condensation beads on every metal surface. The light inside is diffuse and warm, filtered through vegetation.

rooms.name: The Promenade
rooms.description: A wide corridor with a vaulted ceiling, its white tile walls now draped in creeping vines. Faded directional signs in yellow and blue point north to GREENHOUSE and west to AQUAPONICS. The floor tiles have buckled under root pressure, and bioluminescent fungus fills the cracks with thin lines of pale blue-green light, branching like veins across the floor.
aspects: Echoes of a Crew Long Gone

rooms.name: Aquaponics Tanks
rooms.description: Rows of chest-high glass tanks line both walls, most cracked and clouded. Murky green water circulates through corroded pipe connections, driven by some ancient pump that still thumps rhythmically beneath the floor. Pale bioluminescent eels flicker between the intact tanks. Where water drips onto the deck, clusters of white and amber mushrooms sprout from the rust.

rooms.name: The Nursery
rooms.description: Shattered seed trays of brown plastic cover every surface — the floor, the counters, stacked against the walls. Seedlings that were never meant to grow this large have pressed dark-leafed canopies against the ceiling, cracking white panels overhead to expose ductwork and wiring. Thick golden pollen drifts in the warm air like slow snow. A metal workbench near the door is buried under loose papers.

rooms.name: The Greenhouse
rooms.description: The main growing dome, perhaps forty paces across. Transparent panels overhead reveal raw void — black sky and scattered stars behind rivulets of condensation. Below, a riot of green in every shade, from pale lime to deep forest. Chest-high planter beds overflow with vegetation, and narrow paths of swept tile wind between them, recently cleared.

rooms.name: The Canopy
rooms.description: Trees have grown tall enough to press against the dome panels, their dark trunks thick and closely spaced. Vines hang between them like heavy curtains, swaying slightly in air currents from the ventilation system. The light is dim and green-filtered, everything in deep shadow below the canopy. Occasional rustling sounds come from overhead.

rooms.name: Root Wall
rooms.description: The corridor terminates in a wall of interwoven roots, each as thick as a person's torso, bark slick with moisture and dark with age. They pulse with slow green bioluminescence, and through the gaps between them, a deeper, warmer light glows gold. The roots are packed too tightly to squeeze through. The air smells of wet wood and ozone.

features.name: Roots
features.description: Massive interwoven roots, each thicker than your arm, pulsing with slow green bioluminescence. The bark is tough and slick with moisture — too damp to burn. You'd need to coat them with something flammable first.

north.locked_desc: Massive roots block the passage north. They're alive and pulsing with green energy — too thick and damp to cut, too dense to squeeze through.

rooms.name: Observation Platform
rooms.description: A narrow metal platform jutting above the tree canopy, reached by a rusted ladder bolted to the wall. Up here, the dome panels are cracked and missing in places, and the void stretches in every direction — the skerry a faint warm speck of light. Below, the dark green canopy spreads wall to wall, and through it you can trace the root system converging northward like the veins of a leaf.

rooms.name: Growth Control Room
rooms.description: A cramped utility room behind a heavy maintenance hatch, barely large enough for two people. Dead consoles of grey metal and dark glass line three walls. One panel still flickers — its green-on-black screen displaying a schematic of the biodome's root network, most nodes pulsing red. Cables snake across the floor in thick bundles.

features.name: Growth Controller Console
features.description: A flickering console showing a schematic of the biodome's root network. Most nodes pulse red — overgrown, unresponsive. The panel below is dented but not sealed. With the right tools, you could get inside and repair the circuits.

rooms.name: The Verdant Heart
rooms.description: A domed chamber at the biodome's core, every surface threaded with roots. An enormous plant fills the space, crystallized mid-bloom — its petals frozen in a tight spiral of translucent green and gold, refracting light in all directions. The roots radiate outward through the walls and floor like spokes of a wheel. At the base of the crystallized flower, a single living bud the size of a fist pulses with warm amber light.

features.name: Crystallized Bloom
features.description: An enormous flower frozen mid-bloom, petals spiraling outward in crystallized green and gold. It hums faintly. At its base, a single living bud pulses with concentrated energy — small enough to carry, if you could detach it.

enemies_data.name: Tangle-Vine
enemies_data.description: Thick tendrils lash out from the canopy above — a plant that's learned to hunt.
